title: Medicaid
text:
Sources
ALL 2025 Information

General Info
Eligibility



Summary
Though there’s structure and information set up about different tiers of eligibility and premiums, since 2014 in reality it seems like everyone has just used the main eligibility tables. For now, then, we just use this.


Logic
For now, we’re just saying either you DO or DO NOT qualify for medicaid based on household composition by comparing gross monthly income to limit.

title: Tax Calculations
text: All 2025 Information. We do this in a pretty basic fashion: assume standard deductions, apply standard brackets, and then calculate credits.

title: Tax Calculations
text:
Deductions

See here for Federal Deductions
See Page 12 for VT standard deductions, and page 11 for personal exemptions (just the number of people times a constant).

title: Tax Calculations
text:
EITC

See here for Federal EITC tables (the maximum).
See here for listing of all parameters. 
See here for information about Vermont Earned Income Tax Credit. The actual tax documents would not load at time of processing.

title: Three Squares
text:
See https://dcf.vermont.gov/benefits/3SquaresVT for basic information.
See also Snap Eligibility for a more extensive walkthrough.
The Policy Manual is our ultimate source of truth

title: Three Squares
text:
Eligibility
Basically, you can either get Three Squares because gross, or net monthly income: if you are under the limit for your household size.

title: Three Squares
text:
Processing
If eligible, multiply the max benefit for that household size minus 30% of the net monthly income, and that’s your benefit.

title: Child Care Subsidy
text:
Sources

General Info
Max CCFA payouts in 2024



Accepted Service Needs

Option A: Every Caretaker must have at least one of these:

A job
self employed
actively looking for work
attending school or traiing
participating in Reach UP
has special needs

Option B: child has:

health or developmental needs
open case with DCF and child care is part of safety plan

OPTION C: family has:

family is experiencing significant stress in shelter, safety, emotional stability, etc.




Logic

title: Reach Up
text:
Sources

See here for all the rules
See Vermont Legislature Overview for easier to parse numbers.



Overview
Per Vermont Legislature Overview, basically, reach up is:
Basic Needs + Shelter Allowance + Special Housing Allowance (if applicable) = Total needs
Then
Total Needs x Ratable Percentage = Maximum Grant for Family size
Then
Maximum Grant - Countable Income = Reach up Grant
That is
